data.author: cottsay
data.message:
Always prefer 'Tango' icon theme (#250)

The legacy behavior was to attempt to detect if an existing theme was
already set which supplied some of the needed icons, and if not, use the
Tango theme specifically. If that didn't work either, revert the change
to the original theme (or lack thereof).

The current behavior changes the theme only if no theme is set, and only
if tango_icons_vendor is installed.

This change makes qt_gui always prefer the 'Tango' theme, and always
prefer the version supplied by tango_icons_vendor (if available). If
'Tango' is not available, fall back to the system theme.

Signed-off-by: Scott K Logan <logans@cottsay.net>
Signed-off-by: Chris Lalancette <clalancette@openrobotics.org>
Co-authored-by: Chris Lalancette <clalancette@openrobotics.org>

data.author: jacobperron
data.message:
Quiet upstream Qt5 warnings (#210)

* Quiet upstream Qt5 warnings

The warnings are produced by GCC 9 and Qt 5.12.5.
Once the referenced upstream issue has been resolved and patch released, we revert this change.

Signed-off-by: Jacob Perron <jacob@openrobotics.org>

* Condition on GCC 9

Signed-off-by: Jacob Perron <jacob@openrobotics.org>

data.author: homalozoa
data.message:
Fix duplicated QMap to QMultiMap (#244)

‘QMap<K, V>& QMap<K, V>::unite(const QMap<K, V>&) [with Key = QString; T = QString]’ is deprecated. Replace it with QMultiMap.

data.author: fmauch
data.message:
add a qapplication dummy to unittest

The DotToQtGenerator object needs a valid QApplication internally.
Add a dummy one to the unit test makes it run again.

As there might be no x-server available, check for it and skip
the test if no x-server is available.

data.author: Alex Bencz
data.message:
Don't allow floating of plugins via double-click

When -f flag is set, plugins should not be floatable. Clear the
DockWidgetFloatable flag so that a plugin can't be floated by double
clicking.
